preamble: Palatalization of the voiced velar stop or fricative [g/ɣ] often results in the formation of the palatal glide [j] (y).

examples.note: In some Kabyle and Central Moroccan Berber varieties, lax /g/ adjacent to front vowels can further palatalize and weaken to the palatal glide [j] or voiced palatal fricative [ʝ]. This extends the Northern Berber spirantization chain: /g/ → [ɣ] → [ʝ]/[j] in the most advanced dialects.

Central Atlas Tamazight dialects (northern Morocco and northern Algeria) exhibit g → [ʝ] or [j] as the most advanced stage of spirantization, documented by Kossmann (1997). The extent of this shift varies dialectally; High Atlas varieties are more conservative.

sources: Kossmann, M. G. & Stroomer, H. J. (1997). Berber Phonology. In Kaye (ed.), Phonologies of Asia and Africa. Eisenbrauns.
